document.title: GUIDANCE FOR THE EVALUATION OF RISK MANAGEMENT IN MEDICAL ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT
authorityOrJurisdiction: IEC System for Conformity Testing and Certification of Electrotechnical Equipment and Components
standards: IECEE - CB Scheme - IEC 60601-1, Third Edition TRF

sourceSummary.oneLine: IECEE OD-2044-Ed.1.0 gives guidance for evaluating risk management in medical electrical equipment under IEC 60601-1:2005 and ISO 14971:2000 within CB Scheme investigations.
sourceSummary.officialUse: To give a uniform approach to the CBTL and manufacturer on how to assess and document compliance with IEC 60601-1:2005 clauses related to ISO 14971:2000.
keyPoints: IEC 60601-1 third edition incorporates risk management principles according to ISO 14971 within the CB Scheme scope.
A CB Test Certificate does not solely establish legal market entry, but may help substantiate a request for legal market access.
There can be no IEC 60601-1 third edition investigation without the manufacturer's Risk Management File being available.
The CB Test Report and Certificate confirm a risk management process performed for IEC 60601-1 third edition requirements, but do not mean a complete ISO 14971 risk management system is in place.
The guidance includes an IEC 60601-1:2005 / ISO 14971:2000 clause mapping guide and an informative ISO 14971 checklist.

concepts.name: Risk Management Process

evidence.claim: The document is IECEE OD-2044 Edition 1.0 on risk management evaluation in medical electrical equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: IECEE OD-2044-Ed.1.0 OPERATIONAL & RULING DOCUMENTS GUIDANCE FOR THE EVALUATION OF RISK MANAGEMENT IN MEDICAL ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT
OD-2044-Ed.1.0 IEC - IECEE 2010 - Copyright 2010-12-06 all rights reserved

evidence.claim: The guidance scope is a uniform approach for CBTLs and manufacturers to assess and document IEC 60601-1:2005 compliance related to ISO 14971:2000.
evidence.evidenceText: The scope of these guidelines is to give a uniform approach to the CBTL and Manufacturer on how to assess and document compliance with the relevant clauses of IEC 60601-1:2005 related to ISO 14971:2000.

evidence.claim: The reference documents include IEC 60601-1:2005, ISO 14971:2000, and the IECEE CB Scheme IEC 60601-1 Third Edition TRF.
evidence.evidenceText: Reference documents IEC 60601-1:2005 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance ISO 14971:2000, Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices IECEE - CB Scheme – IEC 60601-1, Third Edition TRF

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 third edition is described as incorporating ISO 14971 risk management principles in the CB Scheme scope.
evidence.evidenceText: It is the first IEC standard in the scope of the CB Scheme that incorporates risk management principles according to ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: A CB Test Certificate does not alone establish legal market entry.
evidence.evidenceText: The existence of a CB Test Certificate does not solely establish legal market entry. However, it could be used to help substantiate a request for legal market access.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 third edition investigations require the manufacturer's Risk Management File to be available.
evidence.evidenceText: There can be no investigation to IEC 60601-1 third edition without the manufacturer’s Risk Management File being available.

evidence.claim: ISO 13485 registration is not sufficient to demonstrate an ISO 14971-compliant risk management process.
evidence.evidenceText: The registration to ISO 13485 is not sufficient to demonstrate that a risk management process compliant with ISO 14971 requirements is performed.

evidence.claim: The CB Test Report and Certificate do not imply a complete ISO 14971 risk management system or an audit of the manufacturer's risk management system.
evidence.evidenceText: This does not mean that a complete Risk Management System in compliance with ISO 14971 is in place. A CB Scheme Certificate does not imply that an audit of the manufacturer’s Risk Management System was conducted.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 clause 4.2 does not require post-market monitoring of risk control effectiveness under ISO 14971:2000 clause 9.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.2 of IEC 60601-1 3rd edition does not require post market monitoring (e.g. clause 9 of ISO 14971:2000) of the effectiveness of the risk control measures

evidence.claim: The guidance maps IEC 60601-1:2005 clauses requiring risk management to applicable ISO 14971 clauses.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 1 (appended) provides mapping with all the clauses of IEC 60601-1:2005 which require risk management, and the applicable clauses in ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: Collateral and particular standards containing risk management requirements are to be considered normative with the general standard.
evidence.evidenceText: Collateral and particular standards containing risk management requirements shall be considered together with the general standard as normative.

evidence.claim: The document includes an informative ISO 14971 checklist covering management responsibilities, risk management plan, risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk reduction, residual risk evaluation, and overall risk evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment No. 1 ISO 14971 Checklist (Informative) Clause Title Item References 3.3 Management Responsibilities ... 7 Overall risk evaluation
